meta.ogDesc: Quince leads on 34 pts after 72/104 matches

story: Lording it at the top, Quince is boring everyone into an early grave — 6 clean sheets, zero entertainment. The kind of football that makes people emigrate. Smugly in tow, Sid has turned the goalless draw into an art form nobody asked for — 6 of them. A black hole where fun goes to die. Rounding out the insufferable elite, Big Pewerk's lot defend like the door's been left on the latch, but they'll at least outscore the misery — 29 goals (GD -1), led by Netherlands (beautiful football, then a fistfight among themselves). Thrilling, doomed.

Wallowing in mid-table sludge, Ellie's defence is a public health hazard — 28 shipped, Qatar (lavishly funded, modestly threatening) leaking 10 of them. Genuinely embarrassing to be associated with. Equally forgettable, Etan will be replaying Senegal (power, pace and swagger for days)'s 5-0 all tournament because, let's be honest, it's the only good thing their team will ever do. Frame it. Barely clinging to relevance, Ike saw something in Uzbekistan (the tournament's great unmarked mystery box) that no scout, coach or sane human ever has — played, did nothing, and is somehow still the jewel of that abysmal squad.

Down in the dregs, Murray had to sit and watch New Zealand (the All Whites: good time, not a long time) get battered 1-5 — and we'll be bringing it up at every opportunity for the rest of their natural life. And scraping the very bottom of the barrel, Elsa spread their picks out of sheer cowardice — 4 teams all contributing scraps, not one brave enough to actually be good. Death by committee.

The cull. 17 teams have gone home, the biggest scalp so far being South Africa (Bafana Bafana, big on noise, shy on goals). Etan and Quince carry the most into the knockouts with 5 of six still breathing.

Into the knockouts proper: Ellie's Canada are through and waiting on an opponent. Spare a thought for Murray, who owns both Brazil and Japan in the same R32 tie — a guaranteed win and a guaranteed funeral. Ellie's Germany versus Etan's Paraguay is the pick of the all-in-the-family R32 grudge matches.
